documentation: :Attribute: ScalingConfigurationIds: The list of scaling configuration IDs.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingConfigurations.attr_scaling_configurations
documentation: :Attribute: ScalingConfigurations: The list of scaling configurations.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingConfigurations.refresh_options
documentation:
:Property:

refreshOptions: The refresh strategy for the datasource resource when the stack is updated. Valid values:

- Never: Never refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
- Always: Always refresh the datasource resource when the stack is updated.
Default is Never.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingConfigurations.scaling_configuration_ids
documentation: :Property: scalingConfigurationIds: The ID of scaling configuration that you want to query. Valid values : 1 to 10. The IDs of active and inactive scaling configurations are displayed in the query results, and can be differentiated by LifecycleState.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingConfigurations.scaling_configuration_names
documentation: :Property: scalingConfigurationNames: The name of scaling configuration that you want to query. Valid values : 1 to 10. The names of inactive scaling configurations are not displayed in the query results, and no error is reported.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingConfigurations.scaling_group_id
documentation: :Property: scalingGroupId: The ID of the scaling group. You can use the ID to query all scaling configurations in the scaling group.

This class is a base encapsulation around the ROS resource type `DATASOURCE::ESS::ScalingConfigurations`, which is used to query the information about scaling configurations.

```
Note

```

This class does not contain additional functions, so it is recommended to use the `ScalingConfigurations` class instead of this class for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/datasource-ess-scalingconfigurations>

groupType: The type of instances that are managed by the scaling group. Valid values:
ECS: the Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instances
ECI: the elastic container instances
Default value: ECS.

documentation: :Property: scalingGroupNames: The name of scaling group that you want to query. Valid values: 1 to 20. The names of inactive scaling groups are not displayed in the query results, and no error is reported.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.ROS_RESOURCE_TYPE_NAME
documentation: The resource type name for this resource class.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_adjustment_type
documentation: :Attribute: AdjustmentType: The adjustment method of the scaling rule.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_adjustment_value
documentation: :Attribute: AdjustmentValue: The number of instances that must be scaled based on the scaling rule.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_alarms
documentation: :Attribute: Alarms: The event-triggered tasks that are associated with the scaling rule. The value of this parameter is returned only if you set ShowAlarmRules to true. Otherwise, null is returned.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_cooldown
documentation: :Attribute: Cooldown: The cooldown period of the scaling rule.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_disable_scale_in
documentation: :Attribute: DisableScaleIn: Indicates whether scale-in is disabled.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_estimated_instance_warmup
documentation: :Attribute: EstimatedInstanceWarmup: The warm-up period of instances.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_initial_max_size
documentation: :Attribute: InitialMaxSize: The maximum number of ECS instances that can be contained in the scaling group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_max_size
documentation: :Attribute: MaxSize: The maximum number of ECS instances that can be contained in the scaling group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_metric_name
documentation: :Attribute: MetricName: The name of the metric of the event-triggered task that is associated with the scaling rule.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_min_adjustment_magnitude
documentation: :Attribute: MinAdjustmentMagnitude: The minimum number of instances that must be scaled.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_min_size
documentation: :Attribute: MinSize: The minimum number of ECS instances that must be contained in the scaling group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_predictive_scaling_mode
documentation: :Attribute: PredictiveScalingMode: The mode of the predictive scaling rule.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_predictive_task_buffer_time
documentation: :Attribute: PredictiveTaskBufferTime: The amount of buffer time before prediction tasks are executed.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_predictive_value_behavior
documentation: :Attribute: PredictiveValueBehavior: The action on the predicted maximum value.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_predictive_value_buffer
documentation: :Attribute: PredictiveValueBuffer: The ratio based on which the predicted value is increased when PredictiveValueBehavior is set to PredictiveValueOverrideMaxWithBuffer.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_scale_in_evaluation_count
documentation: :Attribute: ScaleInEvaluationCount: The number of consecutive times that the event-triggered task for scale-in purposes must meet the threshold conditions before an alert is triggered.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_scale_out_evaluation_count
documentation: :Attribute: ScaleOutEvaluationCount: The number of consecutive times that the event-triggered task created for scale-out purposes must meet the threshold conditions before an alert is triggered.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_scaling_group_id
documentation: :Attribute: ScalingGroupId: The ID of the scaling group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRule.attr_scaling_rule_ari
documentation: :Attribute: ScalingRuleAri: The unique identifier of the scaling rule.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRules.scaling_rule_type
documentation:
:Property:

scalingRuleType: The type of the scaling rule. Valid values:
SimpleScalingRule: adjusts the number of ECS instances based on the values of the AdjustmentType and AdjustmentValue parameters.
TargetTrackingScalingRule: calculates the number of ECS instances that need to be scaled in a dynamic manner and maintains the value of a predefined metric close to the value of the TargetValue parameter.
StepScalingRule: scales ECS instances in steps based on the specified thresholds and metric values.
PredictiveScalingRule: uses machine learning to analyze historical monitoring data of the scaling group and predicts the future values of metrics. In addition, Auto Scaling automatically creates scheduled tasks to adjust the boundary values for the scaling group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.datasource.RosScalingRules.show_alarm_rules
documentation:
:Property:

showAlarmRules: Specifies whether to return the event-triggered tasks that are associated with the scaling rule. Valid values:
true
false
Default value: false.

This class is a base encapsulation around the ROS resource type `DATASOURCE::ESS::ScalingRules`.

```
Note

```

This class does not contain additional functions, so it is recommended to use the `ScalingRules` class instead of this class for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/datasource-ess-scalingrules>

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.AlarmTask.attr_alarm_task_id
documentation: Attribute AlarmTaskId: The alarm task ID.

This class encapsulates and extends the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::ESS::AlarmTask`, which is used to create a metric-based alarm task.

```
Note

```

This class may have some new functions to facilitate development, so it is recommended to use this class instead of RosAlarmTask\`\`for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-ess-alarmtask>

classes.name: ros_cdk_ess.AlarmTaskEnable

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.AlarmTaskEnable.__init__
documentation:
Param scope - scope in which this resource is defined Param id - scoped id of the resource Param props - resource properties.

```
param scope



param id



param props



param enable_resource_property_constraint

```

This class encapsulates and extends the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::ESS::AlarmTaskEnable`, which is used to start an alarm task.

You can call this operation to enable alarm tasks when the task is stopped.

```
Note

```

This class may have some new functions to facilitate development, so it is recommended to use this class instead of RosAlarmTaskEnable\`\`for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-ess-alarmtaskenable>

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.EciScalingConfiguration.attr_scaling_configuration_id
documentation: Attribute ScalingConfigurationId: The ID of the elastic container instance.

This class encapsulates and extends the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::ESS::EciScalingConfiguration`.

```
Note

```

This class may have some new functions to facilitate development, so it is recommended to use this class instead of RosEciScalingConfiguration\`\`for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-ess-eciscalingconfiguration>

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.EciScalingConfigurationProps.__init__
documentation:
Properties for defining a `EciScalingConfiguration`.

See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-ess-eciscalingconfiguration>

```
param container_group_name

Property containerGroupName: The name of the elastic container instance.

param scaling_configuration_name

Property scalingConfigurationName: The name of the scaling configuration. The name must be 2 to 64 characters in length and can contain letters, digits, underscores (_), hyphens (-), and periods (.). The name must start with a letter or a digit. The name of the scaling configuration must be unique in a region. If you do not specify this parameter, the scaling configuration ID is used.

param scaling_group_id

Property scalingGroupId: The ID of the scaling group for which you want to create the scaling configuration.

param security_group_id

Property securityGroupId: The ID of the security group with which you want to associate the elastic container instance. Elastic container instances that are associated with the same security group can access each other. If you do not specify a security group, the system uses the default security group in the region that you selected. Make sure that the inbound rules of the security group contain the protocols and the port numbers of the containers that you want to expose. If you do not have a default security group in the region, the system creates a default security group and adds the declared container protocols and port numbers to the inbound rules of the security group.

param acr_registry_infos

Property acrRegistryInfos:.

param active_deadline_seconds

Property containers:.

param data_cache_bucket

param data_cache_pl

Property dataCachePl: The performance level (PL) of the disk used for data caching. We recommend that you use ESSDs. Valid values if you use ESSDs: PL0: An ESSD can provide up to 10,000 random read/write IOPS. PL1: An ESSD can provide up to 50,000 random read/write IOPS. PL2: An ESSD can provide up to 100,000 random read/write IOPS. PL3: An ESSD can provide up to 1,000,000 random read/write IOPS. Default value: PL1. Note For more information about ESSDs, see ESSDs.

param data_cache_provisioned_iops

Property dataCacheProvisionedIops: The IOPS provisioned for the ESSD AutoPL disk used for data caching. Valid values: 0 to min{50000, 1000 × Capacity - Baseline IOPS}, where Baseline IOPS = min{1800 + 50 × Capacity - 50000}. Note For more information about ESSD AutoPL disks, see ESSD AutoPL disks.

This parameter takes effect only if you set CostOptimization to true. Valid values: EntryLevel: entry level (shared instance types) Instance types of this level are the most cost-effective but may not provide stable computing performance. Instance types of this level are suitable for scenarios in which CPU utilization is low. For more information, see Shared instance families. EnterpriseLevel: enterprise level. Instance types of this level provide stable performance and dedicated resources and are suitable for business scenarios that require high stability. For more information, see Overview of instance families. CreditEntryLevel: credit entry level (burstable instance types). CPU credits are used to ensure computing performance. Instance types of this level are suitable for business scenarios in which CPU utilization is low but may fluctuate in specific scenarios. For more information, see Overview of burstable instances.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.EciScalingConfigurationProps.security_group_id
documentation:
Property securityGroupId: The ID of the security group with which you want to associate the elastic container instance.

Elastic container instances that are associated with the same security group can access each other.
If you do not specify a security group, the system uses the default security group in the region that you selected. Make sure that the inbound rules of the security group contain the protocols and the port numbers of the containers that you want to expose. If you do not have a default security group in the region, the system creates a default security group and adds the declared container protocols and port numbers to the inbound rules of the security group.

documentation: Attribute LifecycleHookId: The lifecycle hook ID.

documentation: Attribute ScalingGroupId: The id of the scaling group to which the lifecycle hook belongs.

This class encapsulates and extends the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::ESS::LifecycleHook`, which is used to create a lifecycle hook for a scaling group.

```
Note

```

This class may have some new functions to facilitate development, so it is recommended to use this class instead of RosLifecycleHook\`\`for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-ess-lifecyclehook>

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.LifecycleHookProps.__init__
documentation:
Properties for defining a `LifecycleHook`.

See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-ess-lifecyclehook>

```
param lifecycle_transition

Property lifecycleTransition: The scaling activities to which lifecycle hooks apply Value range: SCALE_OUT: scale-out event SCALE_IN: scale-in event.

param scaling_group_id

Property scalingGroupId: The ID of the scaling group.

param default_result

Property defaultResult: The action that the scaling group takes when the lifecycle hook times out. Value range: CONTINUE: the scaling group continues with the scale-in or scale-out process. ABANDON: the scaling group stops any remaining action of the scale-in or scale-out event. Default value: CONTINUE If the scaling group has multiple lifecycle hooks and one of them is terminated by the DefaultResult=ABANDON parameter during a scale-in event (SCALE_IN), the remaining lifecycle hooks under the same scaling group will also be terminated. Otherwise, the action following the wait state is the next action, as specified in the parameter DefaultResult, after the last lifecycle event under the same scaling group.

param heartbeat_timeout

Property heartbeatTimeout: The time, in seconds, that can elapse before the lifecycle hook times out. If the lifecycle hook times out, the scaling group performs the default action (DefaultResult). The range is from 30 to 86400 seconds. The default value is 600 seconds. You can prevent the lifecycle hook from timing out by calling the RecordLifecycleActionHeartbeat operation. You can also terminate the lifecycle action by calling the CompleteLifecycleAction operation.

param lifecycle_hook_name

Property lifecycleHookName: The name of the lifecycle hook. Each name must be unique within a scaling group. The name must be 2 to 64 characters in length and can contain letters, numbers, Chinese characters, and special characters including underscores (_), hyphens (-) and periods (.). Default value: Lifecycle Hook ID

param notification_arn

Property notificationArn: The Alibaba Cloud Resource Name (ARN) of the notification target that Auto Scaling will use to notify you when an instance is in the transition state for the lifecycle hook. This target can be either an MNS queue or an MNS topic. The format of the parameter value is acs:ess:{region}:{account-id}:{resource-relative-id}. region: the region to which the scaling group locates account-id: Alibaba Cloud ID For example: MNS queue: acs:ess:{region}:{account-id}:queue/{queuename} MNS topic: acs:ess:{region}:{account-id}:topic/{topicname} OOS template: acs:ess:{region}:{account-id}:oos/{templatename}

param notification_metadata

Property notificationMetadata: The fixed string that you want to include when Auto Scaling sends a message about the wait state of the scaling activity to the notification target. The length of the parameter can be up to 4096 characters. Auto Scaling will send the specified NotificationMetadata parameter along with the notification message so that you can easily categorize your notifications. The NotificationMetadata parameter will only take effect after you specify the NotificationArn parameter.
```

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.LifecycleHookProps.lifecycle_transition
documentation: Property lifecycleTransition: The scaling activities to which lifecycle hooks apply Value range:   SCALE_OUT: scale-out event   SCALE_IN: scale-in event.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.LifecycleHookProps.scaling_group_id
documentation: Property scalingGroupId: The ID of the scaling group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.LifecycleHookProps.default_result
documentation:
Property defaultResult: The action that the scaling group takes when the lifecycle hook times out.

Value range:
CONTINUE: the scaling group continues with the scale-in or scale-out process.
ABANDON: the scaling group stops any remaining action of the scale-in or scale-out event.
Default value: CONTINUE
If the scaling group has multiple lifecycle hooks and one of them is terminated by the DefaultResult=ABANDON parameter during a scale-in event (SCALE_IN), the remaining lifecycle hooks under the same scaling group will also be terminated. Otherwise, the action following the wait state is the next action, as specified in the parameter DefaultResult, after the last lifecycle event under the same scaling group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.LifecycleHookProps.heartbeat_timeout
documentation:
Property heartbeatTimeout: The time, in seconds, that can elapse before the lifecycle hook times out.

If the lifecycle hook times out, the scaling group performs the default action (DefaultResult). The range is from 30 to 86400 seconds. The default value is 600 seconds.
You can prevent the lifecycle hook from timing out by calling the RecordLifecycleActionHeartbeat operation. You can also terminate the lifecycle action by calling the CompleteLifecycleAction operation.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.LifecycleHookProps.lifecycle_hook_name
documentation:
Property lifecycleHookName: The name of the lifecycle hook.

Each name must be unique within a scaling group. The name must be 2 to 64 characters in length and can contain letters, numbers, Chinese characters, and special characters including underscores (_), hyphens (-) and periods (.).
Default value: Lifecycle Hook ID

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.LifecycleHookProps.notification_arn
ros_cdk_ess.LifecycleHookProps.notification_metadata
documentation:
Property notificationMetadata: The fixed string that you want to include when Auto Scaling sends a message about the wait state of the scaling activity to the notification target.

The length of the parameter can be up to 4096 characters. Auto Scaling will send the specified NotificationMetadata parameter along with the notification message so that you can easily categorize your notifications. The NotificationMetadata parameter will only take effect after you specify the NotificationArn parameter.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.attr_scaling_configuration_id
documentation: :Attribute: ScalingConfigurationId: The ID of the elastic container instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.container_group_name
documentation: :Property: containerGroupName: The name of the elastic container instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.scaling_configuration_name
documentation:
:Property:

scalingConfigurationName: The name of the scaling configuration. The name must be 2 to 64 characters in length and can contain letters, digits, underscores (_), hyphens (-), and periods (.). The name must start with a letter or a digit.
The name of the scaling configuration must be unique in a region. If you do not specify this parameter, the scaling configuration ID is used.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.scaling_group_id
documentation: :Property: scalingGroupId: The ID of the scaling group for which you want to create the scaling configuration.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.security_group_id
documentation:
:Property:

securityGroupId: The ID of the security group with which you want to associate the elastic container instance. Elastic container instances that are associated with the same security group can access each other.
If you do not specify a security group, the system uses the default security group in the region that you selected. Make sure that the inbound rules of the security group contain the protocols and the port numbers of the containers that you want to expose. If you do not have a default security group in the region, the system creates a default security group and adds the declared container protocols and port numbers to the inbound rules of the security group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.acr_registry_infos
documentation: :Property: acrRegistryInfos:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.active_deadline_seconds
documentation: :Property: activeDeadlineSeconds: The validity period of the scaling configuration. Unit: seconds.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.auto_create_eip
documentation: :Property: autoCreateEip: Specifies whether to automatically create an elastic IP address (EIP) and bind the EIP to the elastic container instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.auto_match_image_cache
documentation:
:Property:

autoMatchImageCache: Specifies whether to automatically match the image cache. Valid values:
true
false
Default value: false.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.containers
documentation: :Property: containers:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.cost_optimization
documentation:
:Property:

costOptimization: Specifies whether to enable the Cost Optimization feature. Valid values:
true
false
Default value: false.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.cpu
documentation: :Property: cpu: The number of vCPUs that you want to allocate to the elastic container instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.cpu_options_core
documentation: :Property: cpuOptionsCore: The number of physical CPU cores. You can specify this parameter for only specific instance types. For more information, see Specify custom CPU options.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.cpu_options_threads_per_core
documentation: :Property: cpuOptionsThreadsPerCore: The number of threads per core. You can specify this parameter for only specific instance types. If you set this parameter to 1, Hyper-Threading is disabled. For more information, see Specify custom CPU options.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.data_cache_bucket
documentation: :Property: dataCacheBucket: The bucket that stores data caches.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.data_cache_bursting_enabled
documentation:
:Property:

dataCacheBurstingEnabled: Specifies whether to enable the Performance Burst feature for the ESSD AutoPL disk used for data caching. Valid values:
true
false
Default value: false.
Note
For more information about ESSD AutoPL disks, see ESSD AutoPL disks.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.data_cache_pl
documentation:
:Property:

dataCachePl: The performance level (PL) of the disk used for data caching. We recommend that you use ESSDs. Valid values if you use ESSDs:
PL0: An ESSD can provide up to 10,000 random read/write IOPS.
PL1: An ESSD can provide up to 50,000 random read/write IOPS.
PL2: An ESSD can provide up to 100,000 random read/write IOPS.
PL3: An ESSD can provide up to 1,000,000 random read/write IOPS.
Default value: PL1.
Note
For more information about ESSDs, see ESSDs.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.data_cache_provisioned_iops
documentation:
:Property:

dataCacheProvisionedIops: The IOPS provisioned for the ESSD AutoPL disk used for data caching. Valid values: 0 to min{50000, 1000 × Capacity - Baseline IOPS}, where Baseline IOPS = min{1800 + 50 × Capacity - 50000}.
Note
For more information about ESSD AutoPL disks, see ESSD AutoPL disks.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.dns_config_name_servers
documentation: :Property: dnsConfigNameServers: The IP addresses of the DNS servers.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.dns_config_options
documentation: :Property: dnsConfigOptions:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.dns_config_searches
documentation: :Property: dnsConfigSearches: The search domains of the DNS server.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.dns_policy
documentation:
:Property:

dnsPolicy: The Domain Name System (DNS) policy. Valid values:
None: uses the DNS that is specified by DnsConfig.
Default: uses the DNS that is specified for the runtime environment.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.egress_bandwidth
documentation: :Property: egressBandwidth: The maximum outbound bandwidth. Unit: bytes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.eip_bandwidth
documentation: :Property: eipBandwidth: The bandwidth of the EIP. Default value: 5. Unit: Mbit/s.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ephemeral_storage
documentation: :Property: ephemeralStorage: The size of the temporary storage space. By default, an enhanced SSD (ESSD) of the PL1 level is used. Unit: GiB.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.host_aliases
documentation: :Property: hostAliases:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.host_name

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.image_registry_credentials
documentation: :Property: imageRegistryCredentials:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.image_snapshot_id
documentation: :Property: imageSnapshotId: The ID of the image cache snapshot.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ingress_bandwidth
documentation: :Property: ingressBandwidth: The maximum inbound bandwidth. Unit: bytes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.init_containers
documentation: :Property: initContainers:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.instance_family_level
documentation:
:Property:

instanceFamilyLevel: The level of the instance family. You can use this parameter to filter instance types that meet the specified criteria. This parameter takes effect only if you set CostOptimization to true. Valid values:
EntryLevel: entry level (shared instance types) Instance types of this level are the most cost-effective but may not provide stable computing performance. Instance types of this level are suitable for scenarios in which CPU utilization is low. For more information, see Shared instance families.
EnterpriseLevel: enterprise level. Instance types of this level provide stable performance and dedicated resources and are suitable for business scenarios that require high stability. For more information, see Overview of instance families.
CreditEntryLevel: credit entry level (burstable instance types). CPU credits are used to ensure computing performance. Instance types of this level are suitable for business scenarios in which CPU utilization is low but may fluctuate in specific scenarios. For more information, see Overview of burstable instances.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.instance_types
documentation: :Property: instanceTypes: The specified ECS instance types. You can specify up to five ECS instance types. For more information, see Specify ECS instance types to create an elastic container instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ipv6_address_count
documentation: :Property: ipv6AddressCount: The number of IPv6 addresses.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.load_balancer_weight
documentation:
:Property:

loadBalancerWeight: The weight of the elastic container instance as a backend server. Valid values: 1 to 100.
Default value: 50.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.memory
documentation: :Property: memory: The memory size that you want to allocate to the elastic container instance. Unit: GiB.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ntp_servers
documentation: :Property: ntpServers: The Network Time Protocol (NTP) server.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ram_role_name
documentation: :Property: ramRoleName: The name of the Resource Access Management (RAM) role that you want to assign to the elastic container instance. Elastic container instances and Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instances can share the same RAM role. For more information, see Use an instance RAM role by calling API operations.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.resource_group_id
documentation: :Property: resourceGroupId: The ID of the resource group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.restart_policy
documentation:
:Property:

restartPolicy: The restart policy of the elastic container instance. Valid values:
Always: always restarts the elastic container instance.
Never: never restarts the elastic container instance.
OnFailure: restarts the elastic container instance upon failures.
Default value: Always.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.security_context_sysctls
documentation: :Property: securityContextSysctls:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.spot_price_limit
documentation:
:Property:

spotPriceLimit: The maximum hourly price of the preemptible elastic container instance. The value can be accurate to three decimal places.
If you set SpotStrategy to SpotWithPriceLimit, you must specify SpotPriceLimit.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.spot_strategy
documentation:
:Property:

spotStrategy: The bidding policy of the instance. Valid values:
NoSpot: The instance is created as a pay-as-you-go instance.
SpotWithPriceLimit: The instance is created as a preemptible instance with a user-defined maximum hourly price.
SpotAsPriceGo: The instance is created as a preemptible instance for which the market price at the time of purchase is used as the bid price.
Default value: NoSpot.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.tags
documentation: :Property: tags:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.termination_grace_period_seconds
documentation: :Property: terminationGracePeriodSeconds: The buffer period during which a program handles operations before the program is stopped. Unit: seconds.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.volumes
documentation: :Property: volumes:

documentation: :Property: name: The image name of the container.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.args
documentation: :Property: args: The container startup arguments. You can specify up to 10 arguments.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.commands
documentation: :Property: commands: The container startup commands. You can specify up to 20 commands. Each command can contain up to 256 characters.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.cpu
documentation: :Property: cpu: The number of CPU cores that you want to allocate to the container.

documentation: :Property: environmentVars:

documentation: :Property: gpu: The number of GPUs that you want to allocate to the container.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.image_pull_policy
documentation:
:Property:

imagePullPolicy: The image pulling policy. Valid values:
Always: Each time instances are created, image pulling is performed.
IfNotPresent: Image pulling is performed as needed. On-premises images are preferentially used. If no on-premises images are available, image pulling is performed.
Never: Image pulling is not performed. On-premises images are always used.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.liveness_probe_exec_commands
documentation: :Property: livenessProbeExecCommands: The command that you want to run by using the CLI in the container to perform liveness probes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.liveness_probe_failure_threshold
documentation:
:Property:

livenessProbeFailureThreshold: The minimum number of consecutive failures before a successful liveness probe is considered failed.
Default value: 3.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.liveness_probe_http_get_path
documentation: :Property: livenessProbeHttpGetPath: The path to which HTTP GET requests are sent when you use the HTTP GET requests to perform liveness probes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.liveness_probe_http_get_port
documentation: :Property: livenessProbeHttpGetPort: The port to which HTTP GET requests are sent when you use the HTTP GET requests to perform liveness probes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.liveness_probe_http_get_scheme
documentation:
:Property:

livenessProbeHttpGetScheme: The protocol type of the HTTP GET requests that you use to perform liveness probes. Valid values:
HTTP
HTTPS

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.liveness_probe_initial_delay_seconds
documentation: :Property: livenessProbeInitialDelaySeconds: The number of seconds that elapses from the startup of the container to the start time of a liveness probe. Unit: seconds.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.liveness_probe_period_seconds
documentation: :Property: livenessProbePeriodSeconds: The interval at which liveness probes are performed. Unit: seconds. Default value: 10. Minimum value: 1.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.liveness_probe_success_threshold
documentation: :Property: livenessProbeSuccessThreshold: The minimum number of consecutive successes before a failed liveness probe is considered successful. Default value: 1. Set the value to 1.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.liveness_probe_tcp_socket_port
documentation: :Property: livenessProbeTcpSocketPort: The port detected by TCP sockets when you use the TCP sockets to perform liveness probes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.liveness_probe_timeout_seconds
documentation: :Property: livenessProbeTimeoutSeconds: The timeout period for a liveness probe. Unit: seconds. Default value: 1. Minimum value: 1.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.memory
documentation: :Property: memory: The memory size that you want to allocate to the container. Unit: GiB.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.ports
documentation: :Property: ports:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.readiness_probe_exec_commands
documentation: :Property: readinessProbeExecCommands: The command that you want to run by using the command line interface (CLI) in the container to perform readiness probes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.readiness_probe_failure_threshold
documentation: :Property: readinessProbeFailureThreshold: The minimum number of consecutive failures before a successful readiness probe is considered failed. Default value: 3.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.readiness_probe_http_get_path
documentation: :Property: readinessProbeHttpGetPath: The path to which HTTP GET requests are sent when you use the HTTP GET requests to perform readiness probes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.readiness_probe_http_get_port
documentation: :Property: readinessProbeHttpGetPort: The port to which HTTP GET requests are sent when you use the HTTP GET requests to perform readiness probes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.readiness_probe_http_get_scheme
documentation:
:Property:

readinessProbeHttpGetScheme: The protocol type of the HTTP GET requests that you use to perform readiness probes. Valid values:
HTTP
HTTPS

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.readiness_probe_initial_delay_seconds
documentation: :Property: readinessProbeInitialDelaySeconds: The number of seconds that elapses from the startup of the container to the start time of a readiness probe. Unit: seconds.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.readiness_probe_period_seconds
documentation: :Property: readinessProbePeriodSeconds: The interval at which readiness probes are performed. Unit: seconds. Default value: 10. Minimum value: 1.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.readiness_probe_success_threshold
documentation: :Property: readinessProbeSuccessThreshold: The minimum number of consecutive successes before a failed readiness probe is considered successful. Default value: 1. Set the value to 1.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.readiness_probe_tcp_socket_port
documentation: :Property: readinessProbeTcpSocketPort: The port that is detected by TCP sockets when you use the TCP sockets to perform readiness probes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.readiness_probe_timeout_seconds
documentation: :Property: readinessProbeTimeoutSeconds: The timeout period for a readiness probe. Unit: seconds. Default value: 1. Minimum value: 1.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.security_context_capabilities_add
documentation:
:Property:

securityContextCapabilitiesAdd: The permissions that are granted to the processes in the container. Only NET_ADMIN and NET_RAW are supported.
Note
If you want to use NET_RAW, submit a ticket.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.security_context_run_as_user
documentation: :Property: securityContextRunAsUser: The ID of the user that runs the container.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.stdin
documentation:
:Property:

stdin: Specifies whether the container allocates buffer resources to standard input streams when the container is running. If you do not specify this parameter, an end-of-file (EOF) error occurs.
Default value: false.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.stdin_once
documentation:
:Property:

stdinOnce: Specifies whether standard input streams remain connected during multiple sessions when Container.N.StdinOnce is set to true.
If you set Container.N.StdinOnce to true, standard input streams are connected after the container is started and remain idle until a client is connected to receive data. After the client is disconnected, the standard input streams are also disconnected and remain disconnected until the container is restarted.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.tty
documentation:
:Property:

tty: Specifies whether to enable interaction. Valid values:
true
false
Default value: false.
If the command is a /bin/bash command, set the value to true.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.volume_mounts
documentation: :Property: volumeMounts:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.ContainersProperty.working_dir
documentation: :Property: workingDir: The working directory of the container.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.InitContainerVolumeMountsProperty.name
documentation: :Property: name: The name of the mounted volume.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.InitContainerVolumeMountsProperty.mount_path
documentation: :Property: mountPath: The directory on which the volume is mounted. Data in this directory is overwritten by the data of the volume.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.InitContainerVolumeMountsProperty.mount_propagation
documentation:
:Property:

mountPropagation: The mount propagation setting of the volume. Mount propagation allows volumes that are mounted on one container to be shared with other containers in the same pod, or even with other pods on the same node. Valid values:
None: The volume mount does not receive subsequent mounts that are mounted to the volume or the subdirectories of the volume.
HostToCotainer: The volume mount receives all subsequent mounts that are mounted to the volume or the subdirectories of the volume.
Bidirectional: This value is similar to HostToCotainer. The volume mount receives all subsequent mounts that are mounted to the volume or its subdirectories. In addition, all volume mounts that are mounted on the container are propagated back to the instance and to all containers of all pods that use the same volume.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.InitContainerVolumeMountsProperty.read_only
documentation:
:Property:

readOnly: Specifies whether the mount directory is read-only.
Default value: false.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.InitContainerVolumeMountsProperty.sub_path
documentation: :Property: subPath: The subdirectory of the volume. The pod can mount different directories of the same volume to different directories of the init container.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.VolumeMountsProperty.mount_propagation

:Property:

mountPropagation: The mount propagation setting of the volume. Mount propagation allows volumes that are mounted on one container to be shared with other containers in the same pod, or even with other pods on the same node. Valid values:
None: The volume mount does not receive subsequent mounts that are mounted to the volume or the subdirectories of the volume.
HostToCotainer: The volume mount receives all subsequent mounts that are mounted to the volume or its subdirectories.
Bidirectional: This value is similar to HostToCotainer. The volume mount receives all subsequent mounts that are mounted to the volume or its subdirectories. In addition, all volume mounts that are mounted on the container are propagated back to the instance and to all containers of all pods that use the same volume.
Default value: None.

type: The volume type. Valid values:
EmptyDirVolume: an empty volume.
NFSVolume: a network file system (NFS) volume.
ConfigFileVolume: a configuration file.
FlexVolume: a volume that uses the FlexVolume plug-in to extend storage and supports disks.
HostPathVolume: a file or path of the host node.
DiskVolume: a disk volume. This value is not recommended. We recommend that you set Volume.N.Type to FlexVolume.

documentation: :Property: diskVolumeDiskId: The ID of the DiskVolume-typed volume.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.VolumesProperty.disk_volume_disk_size
documentation: :Property: diskVolumeDiskSize: The storage size of the DiskVolume-typed volume. Unit: GiB.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.VolumesProperty.empty_dir_volume_medium
documentation: :Property: emptyDirVolumeMedium: The storage medium of the EmptyDirVolume-typed volume. If you leave this parameter empty, the file system that backs the node is used as the storage medium. If you set this parameter to memory, the memory is used as the storage medium.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.VolumesProperty.empty_dir_volume_size_limit
documentation: :Property: emptyDirVolumeSizeLimit: The storage size of the EmptyDirVolume-typed volume. Unit: GiB or MiB.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.VolumesProperty.flex_volume_fs_type
documentation: :Property: flexVolumeFsType: The file system type of the FlexVolume-typed volume. The default value is determined by the script of the FlexVolume plug-in.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.VolumesProperty.flex_volume_options
documentation:
:Property:

flexVolumeOptions: The options of the FlexVolume-typed volume. Each option is a key-value pair contained in a JSON string.
When you use the FlexVolume plug-in to mount a disk, specify the options in the {"volumeId":"d-2zehdahrwoa7srg****","performanceLevel": "PL2"} format.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.VolumesProperty.host_path_volume_path
documentation: :Property: hostPathVolumePath: The absolute path on the host.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.VolumesProperty.host_path_volume_type

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.VolumesProperty.nfs_volume_path

true
false
Default value: false.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfiguration.VolumesProperty.nfs_volume_server
documentation: :Property: nfsVolumeServer: The address of the NFS server.

This class is a base encapsulation around the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::ESS::EciScalingConfiguration`.

See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-ess-eciscalingconfiguration>

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.container_group_name
documentation: :Property: containerGroupName: The name of the elastic container instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.scaling_configuration_name
documentation:
:Property:

scalingConfigurationName: The name of the scaling configuration. The name must be 2 to 64 characters in length and can contain letters, digits, underscores (_), hyphens (-), and periods (.). The name must start with a letter or a digit.
The name of the scaling configuration must be unique in a region. If you do not specify this parameter, the scaling configuration ID is used.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.scaling_group_id
documentation: :Property: scalingGroupId: The ID of the scaling group for which you want to create the scaling configuration.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.security_group_id
documentation:
:Property:

securityGroupId: The ID of the security group with which you want to associate the elastic container instance. Elastic container instances that are associated with the same security group can access each other.
If you do not specify a security group, the system uses the default security group in the region that you selected. Make sure that the inbound rules of the security group contain the protocols and the port numbers of the containers that you want to expose. If you do not have a default security group in the region, the system creates a default security group and adds the declared container protocols and port numbers to the inbound rules of the security group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.acr_registry_infos
documentation: :Property: acrRegistryInfos:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.active_deadline_seconds
documentation: :Property: activeDeadlineSeconds: The validity period of the scaling configuration. Unit: seconds.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.auto_create_eip
documentation: :Property: autoCreateEip: Specifies whether to automatically create an elastic IP address (EIP) and bind the EIP to the elastic container instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.auto_match_image_cache
documentation:
:Property:

autoMatchImageCache: Specifies whether to automatically match the image cache. Valid values:
true
false
Default value: false.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.containers
documentation: :Property: containers:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.cost_optimization
documentation:
:Property:

costOptimization: Specifies whether to enable the Cost Optimization feature. Valid values:
true
false
Default value: false.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.cpu
documentation: :Property: cpu: The number of vCPUs that you want to allocate to the elastic container instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.cpu_options_core
documentation: :Property: cpuOptionsCore: The number of physical CPU cores. You can specify this parameter for only specific instance types. For more information, see Specify custom CPU options.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.cpu_options_threads_per_core
documentation: :Property: cpuOptionsThreadsPerCore: The number of threads per core. You can specify this parameter for only specific instance types. If you set this parameter to 1, Hyper-Threading is disabled. For more information, see Specify custom CPU options.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.data_cache_bucket
documentation: :Property: dataCacheBucket: The bucket that stores data caches.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.data_cache_bursting_enabled
documentation:
:Property:

dataCacheBurstingEnabled: Specifies whether to enable the Performance Burst feature for the ESSD AutoPL disk used for data caching. Valid values:
true
false
Default value: false.
Note
For more information about ESSD AutoPL disks, see ESSD AutoPL disks.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.data_cache_pl
documentation:
:Property:

dataCachePl: The performance level (PL) of the disk used for data caching. We recommend that you use ESSDs. Valid values if you use ESSDs:
PL0: An ESSD can provide up to 10,000 random read/write IOPS.
PL1: An ESSD can provide up to 50,000 random read/write IOPS.
PL2: An ESSD can provide up to 100,000 random read/write IOPS.
PL3: An ESSD can provide up to 1,000,000 random read/write IOPS.
Default value: PL1.
Note
For more information about ESSDs, see ESSDs.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.data_cache_provisioned_iops
documentation:
:Property:

dataCacheProvisionedIops: The IOPS provisioned for the ESSD AutoPL disk used for data caching. Valid values: 0 to min{50000, 1000 × Capacity - Baseline IOPS}, where Baseline IOPS = min{1800 + 50 × Capacity - 50000}.
Note
For more information about ESSD AutoPL disks, see ESSD AutoPL disks.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.dns_config_name_servers
documentation: :Property: dnsConfigNameServers: The IP addresses of the DNS servers.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.dns_config_options
documentation: :Property: dnsConfigOptions:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.dns_config_searches
documentation: :Property: dnsConfigSearches: The search domains of the DNS server.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.dns_policy
documentation:
:Property:

dnsPolicy: The Domain Name System (DNS) policy. Valid values:
None: uses the DNS that is specified by DnsConfig.
Default: uses the DNS that is specified for the runtime environment.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.egress_bandwidth
documentation: :Property: egressBandwidth: The maximum outbound bandwidth. Unit: bytes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.eip_bandwidth
documentation: :Property: eipBandwidth: The bandwidth of the EIP. Default value: 5. Unit: Mbit/s.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.ephemeral_storage
documentation: :Property: ephemeralStorage: The size of the temporary storage space. By default, an enhanced SSD (ESSD) of the PL1 level is used. Unit: GiB.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.host_aliases
documentation: :Property: hostAliases:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.host_name
documentation: :Property: hostName: The hostname of the elastic container instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.image_registry_credentials
documentation: :Property: imageRegistryCredentials:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.image_snapshot_id
documentation: :Property: imageSnapshotId: The ID of the image cache snapshot.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.ingress_bandwidth
documentation: :Property: ingressBandwidth: The maximum inbound bandwidth. Unit: bytes.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.init_containers
documentation: :Property: initContainers:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.instance_family_level
documentation:
:Property:

instanceFamilyLevel: The level of the instance family. You can use this parameter to filter instance types that meet the specified criteria. This parameter takes effect only if you set CostOptimization to true. Valid values:
EntryLevel: entry level (shared instance types) Instance types of this level are the most cost-effective but may not provide stable computing performance. Instance types of this level are suitable for scenarios in which CPU utilization is low. For more information, see Shared instance families.
EnterpriseLevel: enterprise level. Instance types of this level provide stable performance and dedicated resources and are suitable for business scenarios that require high stability. For more information, see Overview of instance families.
CreditEntryLevel: credit entry level (burstable instance types). CPU credits are used to ensure computing performance. Instance types of this level are suitable for business scenarios in which CPU utilization is low but may fluctuate in specific scenarios. For more information, see Overview of burstable instances.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.instance_types
documentation: :Property: instanceTypes: The specified ECS instance types. You can specify up to five ECS instance types. For more information, see Specify ECS instance types to create an elastic container instance.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.ipv6_address_count
documentation: :Property: ipv6AddressCount: The number of IPv6 addresses.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.load_balancer_weight
documentation:
:Property:

loadBalancerWeight: The weight of the elastic container instance as a backend server. Valid values: 1 to 100.
Default value: 50.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.memory
documentation: :Property: memory: The memory size that you want to allocate to the elastic container instance. Unit: GiB.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.ntp_servers
documentation: :Property: ntpServers: The Network Time Protocol (NTP) server.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.ram_role_name
documentation: :Property: ramRoleName: The name of the Resource Access Management (RAM) role that you want to assign to the elastic container instance. Elastic container instances and Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instances can share the same RAM role. For more information, see Use an instance RAM role by calling API operations.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.resource_group_id
documentation: :Property: resourceGroupId: The ID of the resource group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.restart_policy
documentation:
:Property:

restartPolicy: The restart policy of the elastic container instance. Valid values:
Always: always restarts the elastic container instance.
Never: never restarts the elastic container instance.
OnFailure: restarts the elastic container instance upon failures.
Default value: Always.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.security_context_sysctls
documentation: :Property: securityContextSysctls:

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.spot_price_limit
documentation:
:Property:

spotPriceLimit: The maximum hourly price of the preemptible elastic container instance. The value can be accurate to three decimal places.
If you set SpotStrategy to SpotWithPriceLimit, you must specify SpotPriceLimit.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosEciScalingConfigurationProps.spot_strategy
documentation:
:Property:

spotStrategy: The bidding policy of the instance. Valid values:
NoSpot: The instance is created as a pay-as-you-go instance.
SpotWithPriceLimit: The instance is created as a preemptible instance with a user-defined maximum hourly price.
SpotAsPriceGo: The instance is created as a preemptible instance for which the market price at the time of purchase is used as the bid price.
Default value: NoSpot.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHook.ROS_RESOURCE_TYPE_NAME
documentation: The resource type name for this resource class.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHook.attr_lifecycle_hook_id
documentation: :Attribute: LifecycleHookId: The lifecycle hook ID

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHook.attr_scaling_group_id
documentation: :Attribute: ScalingGroupId: The id of the scaling group to which the lifecycle hook belongs.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHook.lifecycle_transition
documentation:
:Property:

lifecycleTransition: The scaling activities to which lifecycle hooks apply Value range:
SCALE_OUT: scale-out event
SCALE_IN: scale-in event

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHook.scaling_group_id
documentation: :Property: scalingGroupId: The ID of the scaling group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHook.default_result
documentation:
:Property:

defaultResult: The action that the scaling group takes when the lifecycle hook times out. Value range:
CONTINUE: the scaling group continues with the scale-in or scale-out process.
ABANDON: the scaling group stops any remaining action of the scale-in or scale-out event.
Default value: CONTINUE
If the scaling group has multiple lifecycle hooks and one of them is terminated by the DefaultResult=ABANDON parameter during a scale-in event (SCALE_IN), the remaining lifecycle hooks under the same scaling group will also be terminated. Otherwise, the action following the wait state is the next action, as specified in the parameter DefaultResult, after the last lifecycle event under the same scaling group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHook.heartbeat_timeout
documentation:
:Property:

heartbeatTimeout: The time, in seconds, that can elapse before the lifecycle hook times out. If the lifecycle hook times out, the scaling group performs the default action (DefaultResult). The range is from 30 to 86400 seconds. The default value is 600 seconds.
You can prevent the lifecycle hook from timing out by calling the RecordLifecycleActionHeartbeat operation. You can also terminate the lifecycle action by calling the CompleteLifecycleAction operation.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHook.lifecycle_hook_name
documentation:
:Property:

lifecycleHookName: The name of the lifecycle hook. Each name must be unique within a scaling group. The name must be 2 to 64 characters in length and can contain letters, numbers, Chinese characters, and special characters including underscores (_), hyphens (-) and periods (.).
Default value: Lifecycle Hook ID

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHook.notification_arn
ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHook.notification_metadata
documentation: :Property: notificationMetadata: The fixed string that you want to include when Auto Scaling sends a message about the wait state of the scaling activity to the notification target. The length of the parameter can be up to 4096 characters. Auto Scaling will send the specified NotificationMetadata parameter along with the notification message so that you can easily categorize your notifications. The NotificationMetadata parameter will only take effect after you specify the NotificationArn parameter.

This class is a base encapsulation around the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::ESS::LifecycleHook`, which is used to create a lifecycle hook for a scaling group.

```
Note

```

This class does not contain additional functions, so it is recommended to use the `LifecycleHook` class instead of this class for a more convenient development experience.
See <https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/ros/developer-reference/aliyun-ess-lifecyclehook>

```

defaultResult: The action that the scaling group takes when the lifecycle hook times out. Value range:
CONTINUE: the scaling group continues with the scale-in or scale-out process.

Default value: CONTINUE
If the scaling group has multiple lifecycle hooks and one of them is terminated by the DefaultResult=ABANDON parameter during a scale-in event (SCALE_IN), the remaining lifecycle hooks under the same scaling group will also be terminated. Otherwise, the action following the wait state is the next action, as specified in the parameter DefaultResult, after the last lifecycle event under the same scaling group.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHookProps.heartbeat_timeout
documentation:
:Property:

heartbeatTimeout: The time, in seconds, that can elapse before the lifecycle hook times out. If the lifecycle hook times out, the scaling group performs the default action (DefaultResult). The range is from 30 to 86400 seconds. The default value is 600 seconds.
You can prevent the lifecycle hook from timing out by calling the RecordLifecycleActionHeartbeat operation. You can also terminate the lifecycle action by calling the CompleteLifecycleAction operation.

methods.name: ros_cdk_ess.RosLifecycleHookProps.lifecycle_hook_name
documentation:
:Property:

lifecycleHookName: The name of the lifecycle hook. Each name must be unique within a scaling group. The name must be 2 to 64 characters in length and can contain letters, numbers, Chinese characters, and special characters including underscores (_), hyphens (-) and periods (.).
Default value: Lifecycle Hook ID

weight: The weight of an Elastic Compute Service (ECS) instance as a backend server in the backend server groups of the attached CLB instance. If you increase the weight of an ECS instance, the number of access requests that are forwarded to the ECS instance also increases. If you set Weight to 0 for an ECS instance, no access requests are forwarded to the ECS instance.
Valid values: 0 to 100.

This class is a base encapsulation around the ROS resource type `ALIYUN::ESS::LoadBalancerAttachment`, which is used to add one or more Server Load Balancer (SLB) instances.
